content.full_text:
Office of
the City Council

3232 Main Street
Lemon Grove, CA 91945
(619) 464-6934
Fax 460-3716

1-2-92

Dear MR. WALLACE :

Recently I Sent you a Package of my
Columns with a Covering Explanatory Letter.
I am most interested in a Legal Determination
by your office as soon as possible. I am
continuing to write, and would like to continue
to do so if appropriate by your Commission.

If you need additional information,
please call me — (619) 466-5579 (Home phone)

Thank you for your assistance in this
matter -

Dr. Robert T. Burns
Councilman - City of Lemon Grove.

Printed on recycled paper

The image appears to be a blank or nearly blank page with minimal visible text. There are a few faint marks or lines visible, but no clearly legible text content that can be reliably transcribed.

Fair Political Practises Commission Nov 27 9 54 AM '91
Mr. Wallace, Atty.

11-22-91

Dear Mr. Wallace:

Regarding our conversation on 11-18-91, I am enclosing a few copies of my columns in the Lemon Grove Review, plus a couple of copies of the paper itself. It is circulated only in our City of 23,000 on a weekly basis with some subscriptions and some street sales from coin boxes. I began in Dec. 1990 and have never been paid for the writing. I also have been writing a "Pet Care Column" in another paper in an adjoining City, and have written articles for Veterinary Journals, the American Rifleman Magazine, as well as guest editorials and letters to the Editor. I have taken several college extention courses to improve my writing skills, have dabbled a little in poetry, and would like to compile some of it in a book someday. I think of writing as an avocation and enjoy doing it.

I have lived in Lemon Grove since 1955 and practised Veterinary Medicine until retiring in Dec. of 1990. I have been involved in my community in many ways during that time, being elected to our City Council in 1977 and re elected three times since. My term is up in June of 92 and I hope to be re elected again. I have been writing the columns to do a better job of communication as a Councilman, and inform them of current matters and on the issues. I have entreated everyone to a dialogue that can involve the public and answer questions, plus offer my opinions on the various subjects. Whether I am quizzed in a supermarket or by letter, I feel that I am being more open to the constituents, and expressing myself in writing better than I can verbally. I have also acted as a part time stringer for the newspapers to publicize events that would be missed otherwise, also without compensation.

For the record I want a written opinion from your organization so that I will not be bending any rules of campaigning. Would it be better if I were paid for the columns? Would it be better if the editor offered to publish other opinions or comments ? If I never specifically mention my running for office, or comment on the election in any personal way, would it be proper? ( I certainly do not intend to use it as a forum to get vote for myself.) I would welcome all others to offer their opinions and I am sure that the editor would be happy to cooperate.

2

It seems to me that the adage of "if any one percieives that something is
wrong, then it should not be done" could well preclude anyone from doing
anything if carried out to the absurd extreme. I cannot really see how this
could give me any unfair advantage in that anyone is free to do the same.
If someone just cannot write, or prefers not to, I do not believe that one
who can and does, should be denied. I am not schooled with the mouth such
as Lawyers are, having dealt with dogs and cats most of my career, but I do
not believe that only orators are qualified to be good Councilmen. Quite the
opposite, in my opinion and from my experience, in many cases.

I am hoping that you will give me a go ahead with my columns, and will
be very happy to follow any guidelines or prohibitions that you wish to in-
form me of so that it will be within the intent and purposes of the FPPC
goals. I would like to continue to inform, and would also like to stimulate
a higher voter turnout without any directional pointing. I think that too
many politicians actually hide from the public so they won't have to commit
themselves, or waste time talking to constituents. Screened by an efficient
secretary, and shopping in another town is a great way to accomplish this.
I enjoy circulating around town, lunching at the coffee shop, and meeting
my neighbors and constituents, and my columns are a way of answering the
questions asked by a few, but wondered about by the many.

Lastly, money has absolutely nothing to do with any of this. In the
four campaigns, I have never asked for nor accepted a penny from anyone. My
campaigns are cheap and the leg work is done mostly by my family(five child-
ren, four grandchildren). Also it has acquired the top number of votes cast
in the last three elections, so it works for me.

sincerely [signature]
Dr. Robert F. Burns
2337 Mt. Vernon St.
Lemon Grove, CA. 91945
